reasons: Kaja directly denies Karrie's repeated-attack uptime by suppressing and dragging her before she can safely stack Lightwheel Mark.
This matchup works especially well as a pickoff setup because Karrie is vulnerable when she is pulled away from her peel or frontline.

proof.summary: Karrie wants uninterrupted basic attacks and skill hits to stack Lightwheel Mark, while Kaja can suppress and drag her during the key DPS window before she can freely attack.
worksBestWhen: Kaja saves Divine Judgment for Karrie instead of using it on the frontline.
Kaja reaches Karrie from a flank, brush angle, or Flicker engage.
Teammates follow immediately after Kaja pulls Karrie out of position.
failureCases: Kaja cannot reach Karrie because she stays behind strong peel.
Kaja commits without teammate damage follow-up.
Kaja suppresses the wrong target and Karrie is left free to deal sustained damage.

reasons: Franco can stop Karrie's DPS window with suppression, forcing her to lose the repeated attacks needed to trigger Lightwheel Mark consistently.
His hook and ultimate pressure punish Karrie when she steps forward to attack tanks or objectives.

proof.summary: Karrie needs safe forward positioning to keep attacking, while Franco can punish that position with Bloody Hunt suppression and coordinated burst follow-up.
worksBestWhen: Franco holds Bloody Hunt until Karrie commits to a fight or objective.
Franco threatens Karrie from fog of war or brush control.
The team is ready to burst Karrie during the suppression duration.
failureCases: Franco misses hook and loses map pressure.
Franco suppresses Karrie without teammate follow-up.
Karrie stays out of hook and ultimate range while protected by her frontline.

reasons: Lolita directly reduces Karrie's basic-attack pressure by blocking ranged basic attacks and projectiles with her shield when positioned correctly.
This protects teammates during the time Karrie wants to stack Lightwheel Mark through repeated hits.

proof.summary: Karrie relies on repeated ranged attacks to build pressure, while Lolita can face Karrie and block ranged basic attacks and projectiles to protect the team during Karrie's DPS window.
worksBestWhen: Lolita positions between Karrie and the protected teammate.
Lolita angles Guardian's Bulwark toward Karrie's attack direction.
Allied damage dealers stay behind Lolita's shield line.
failureCases: Karrie attacks from the side or waits out the shield duration.
Lolita shields too early before Karrie commits.
Karrie finds a different target outside Lolita's shield angle.

reasons: Natalia can threaten Karrie before Karrie gets protected DPS uptime in a teamfight.
Smoke Bomb is especially relevant because Karrie depends heavily on basic attacks to apply pressure and stack Lightwheel Mark.

proof.summary: Karrie needs repeated basic attacks to build her damage pattern, while Natalia can enter from vision gaps and use Smoke Bomb to deny basic-attack retaliation during the pickoff window.
worksBestWhen: Natalia finds Karrie before teamfight formation is complete.
Natalia attacks from fog of war, brush, or a flank route.
Karrie's dash or defensive response is unavailable or already used.
failureCases: Karrie stays grouped with peel and vision control.
Natalia enters after Karrie already has protection and damage uptime.
Natalia cannot finish the pickoff before the enemy team collapses.

reasons: Saber can punish Karrie's low durability with single-target airborne burst before she has time to stack true damage.
Saber counters Karrie by forcing a short, controlled burst window with airborne crowd control, which reduces Karrie's chance to reposition, kite, or build repeated Lightwheel Mark stacks.

proof.summary: Karrie wants extended attack uptime to trigger Lightwheel Mark repeatedly, while Saber can force a short burst fight with Triple Sweep before Karrie reaches her full DPS value.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits for Phantom Step or defensive tools to be used.
Saber catches Karrie away from peel or vision.
Saber has enough burst damage to finish Karrie during the crowd-control window.
failureCases: Karrie survives the burst and returns damage with teammate protection.
Saber dives into a protected backline without follow-up.
Karrie has defensive item timing or positioning that prevents Saber from finishing the kill.
